title: desmodium procumbens var. neomexicanum
intro: Desmodium procumbens var. neomexicanum, also known as New Mexican tick clover, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Desmodium procumbens var. neomexicanum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote tissue repair. Desmodium procumbens var. neomexicanum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in folk remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
